title: Structuring chaos

spec.title: Structuring chaos
spec.notes_for_generator: Day 14 final free workout. Dump messy notes into a chat-based LLM and get a structured document. Emphasise anonymisation, ambiguity preservation, and gap-finding. End with a grounded invitation into The 90-Day AI Habit: the paid programme covering applied LLM practice across writing/editing, strategy/decision-making, research/assessment, management/delegation, personal productivity, and tangible outputs. Mention the LinkedIn badge, workplace launch pack, and the pay-what-it-is-worth 90-day route with £49 as the suggested default.

content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 14: Turn messy notes into something usable

You've been in a meeting. You've captured something. But your notes are a wreck: half-sentences, names without context, actions buried under observations, a decision that might be a decision or might just be something someone said. Now someone needs a document.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
───────────────
🗂️ STRUCTURING | 🔴 PUSH | ⏱ 15–20 min

THE BRIEF:
Take a messy set of notes from a real meeting, call, workshop, voice note, or planning session. If the notes include people, clients, patients, money, or anything sensitive, anonymise them first. Your job is not to make the notes sound clever. Your job is to turn them into a structured document that preserves what is known, what is uncertain, and what still needs a decision.

YOUR REPS:
1. Give your chosen chat-based LLM the messy notes and enough context to understand the situation: what the meeting was about, who the document is for, and what format would be useful. Ask it to organise the notes into clear sections such as decisions, actions, risks, open questions, useful context, and follow-ups. Tell it to mark uncertainty rather than smoothing it away.

2. Read the structured version like a sceptical human. Look for anything the model has made too definite: fake decisions, invented priorities, action owners that were only implied, or neat wording that hides ambiguity. Send a follow-up asking it to separate confirmed facts from assumptions and to list the gaps that still need checking.

3. Now ask for two outputs from the same material: a clean version you could send to someone else, and a private action list for you. The public version should be calm and readable. The private version should be brutally useful: owners, next actions, missing information, and decisions still needed.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
• A reader can tell the difference between a confirmed decision, a likely next step, and an unresolved question
• No sensitive names or details have been exposed unnecessarily
• The action list contains specific next moves, not vague verbs like "follow up" without context
• The model has made the mess easier to work with without pretending the mess never existed

💡 COACHING NOTE:
LLMs are very good at imposing structure on chaos. That is useful, but also dangerous. A clean format can make weak information look stronger than it is, so your job is to keep uncertainty visible. This transfers to meeting notes, research dumps, client calls, strategy workshops, and any moment where the first draft is not a blank page but a pile of fragments.

──────────
You have now finished the free 14-day starter block. Fourteen days is enough to get moving; it is rarely enough to make a new working habit automatic. Habit research points to weeks of repetition, often closer to two or three months than two weeks.

If AI Habit has started to work for you, The 90-Day AI Habit is the next level: more reps, harder workflows, and a longer runway to make this part of how you work. It will cover writing and editing, strategy and decision-making, research and assessment, management and delegation, personal productivity, and practical outputs you can keep using.

The aim is not just more prompts. By the end, you should have a working pack: useful artefacts, a clearer AI practice, a LinkedIn-ready completion badge, and a workplace launch pack you could use to bring this into work. You can join by paying what you think the next 76 days are worth to your work and career. The suggested default is £49, but the slider starts at £1 and goes up to £200.

Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive.
Share the programme: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
Day 14 reflection and 90-day interest: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit-day-14/
Found this useful? You can leave a tip: https://ko-fi.com/theaihabit
review_notes: Clarified that AI Habit focuses on chat-based large language models (LLMs), while using AI as public shorthand.
Kept exercises model-agnostic and usable with any text-based LLM or approved work tool.
Reworked research/reasoning language to avoid over-trusting model confidence or hidden chain-of-thought.
Reframed AI Habit as a grounded free 14-day starter block with a paid continuation for deeper 60-90 day habit-building, rather than a validation experiment.
Restored Day 14 as a complete final workout before the paid continuation ask.

Positioned the paid continuation as The 90-Day AI Habit: a structured applied-LLM programme covering writing/editing, strategy/decision-making, research/assessment, management/delegation, personal productivity, and practical outputs.
Simplified the 90-day commercial route: join individually, bring a small group for a discounted group subscription, or use the workplace launch pack to get an employer to pay.
Added ethical upgrade nudges to Days 3, 7, and 10: endowed progress, career identity, and momentum protection for The 90-Day AI Habit.
Mechanical repair pass: normalised workout badge line after scheduler verification.
Updated paid route copy to pay-what-it-is-worth pricing.
